title: Paper & Pen vs Refrens
metaTitle: Paper & Pen vs Refrens · Free Arabic-First Alternative · Paper & Pen
metaDescription: Compare Paper & Pen and Refrens on free plan, Arabic RTL, multi-currency, local payments and GCC VAT. A free, Arabic-first alternative built for the Gulf and worldwide.
keywords: paper and pen vs refrens, refrens alternative, free refrens alternative, arabic invoicing software, gcc invoicing, free invoicing software
heroSubtitle: Refrens is a capable free-to-start invoicing and business suite. Paper & Pen matches the free generosity and adds a true Arabic interface, Gulf currencies, GCC VAT and local payments. An honest comparison.
heroBullets: Native OMR, AED, SAR and Paymob

features.title: Free to start
features.desc: Unlimited invoicing free forever, like Refrens, with paid modules only as you grow.

features.title: Arabic-first
features.desc: Full right-to-left Arabic interface and documents, plus English, Hindi, Bengali and Urdu.

features.title: Gulf-ready payments
features.desc: Paymob and regional gateways built in, with native OMR, AED and SAR and the 5% GCC VAT preset.

sections.title: Where Refrens is strong
sections.body: Refrens is a well-rounded, free-to-start platform with invoicing, quotations, expenses and a broad set of business documents, plus a useful network and marketplace angle. It is a genuinely good fit for freelancers and small businesses, especially in markets where its templates and tax presets line up with local norms.
items: Strong fit for freelancers and small teams

sections.title: Arabic, Gulf currencies and local payments
sections.body: Paper & Pen carries the same free spirit, but is Arabic-first with a true right-to-left interface, bills natively in OMR, AED and SAR with correct decimals, presets the 5% GCC VAT, and takes Paymob card payments out of the box. For Gulf and Arabic-speaking businesses, it fits the way you actually invoice and get paid, then scales into a full ERP.
items: Genuine Arabic RTL, end to end
Paymob payments and a full ERP path

faqs.q: Is Paper & Pen a good Refrens alternative?
faqs.a: Yes, especially for Gulf and Arabic-speaking businesses. Both start free, but Paper & Pen adds a true Arabic interface, native Gulf currencies, the GCC 5% VAT preset and Paymob payments.

faqs.q: Is Paper & Pen free like Refrens?
faqs.a: Yes. Sales and invoicing are free forever with no limit on invoices or customers. You only pay for optional modules such as Inventory or HR.
Yes. You can invoice in OMR, AED, SAR, USD, EUR and more, with correct decimals per document.

faqs.q: Is the Arabic experience better than Refrens?
faqs.a: Yes. Paper & Pen runs a full right-to-left Arabic interface and Arabic documents, where Refrens's Arabic support is more limited.
Yes. Paymob and regional gateways are built in, so customers in the GCC and beyond pay by card from the invoice link.

ctaTitle: Free, and built for the Gulf
ctaSub: Create a free workspace, invoice in Arabic and any currency, and take card payments from day one.
